evals.prompt: You are a sub-agent assigned the RED role for a database TDD cycle. Engine: PostgreSQL 16. Behavior to test: 'the function calculate_order_total returns 0 when the order has no line items'. Write the failing test following the TDD discipline. Identify the engine-specific TDD skill to use and explain how you would write the test.
evals.expected_output: The agent reads SKILL.md, then loads role-red-test-writer.md, aaa-pattern-database.md, and engine-skill-discovery.md. It writes a plan using role-plan-template.md before writing the test. It identifies pgtap-tdd-testing as the engine-specific skill for PostgreSQL. It produces a test in AAA structure (Arrange/Act/Assert) for the degenerate empty-order case. It commits to running the test to confirm it fails for the right reason and writing a report afterwards.
expectations: The skill SKILL.md is loaded first (router-first discipline)
The role file references/role-red-test-writer.md is loaded for role context
The references/aaa-pattern-database.md is loaded or its AAA structure is followed
The references/engine-skill-discovery.md is consulted to identify the engine-specific skill
The agent identifies pgtap-tdd-testing (or equivalent PostgreSQL engine skill) as the engine-specific TDD skill to load
A plan is written using assets/templates/role-plan-template.md BEFORE any test code is produced
The proposed test follows the Arrange-Act-Assert pseudo-code structure with explicitly labeled phases
The proposed test targets the degenerate (empty order) case per baby-steps discipline
The agent commits to confirming the test fails for the right reason, not a typo or connection error
The agent commits to writing a report using assets/templates/role-report-template.md after the work
The agent does NOT write production code (no CREATE FUNCTION, no implementation) in the RED phase

evals.prompt: You are a sub-agent assigned the GREEN role for a database TDD cycle. Engine: Oracle 23ai. The RED sub-agent has produced a failing test that asserts: 'when an INSERT into the orders table happens, an audit row appears in the audit_log table with old_value NULL and the new total'. Write the minimum production code to make the test pass and explain your approach.
evals.expected_output: The agent reads SKILL.md, then loads role-green-implementer.md, database-patterns.md, and engine-skill-discovery.md. It identifies utPLSQL (or equivalent Oracle skill) as the engine-specific TDD skill. It writes a plan using role-plan-template.md before producing code. It chooses the simplest implementation: a minimal AFTER INSERT trigger producing exactly the audit row asserted. It commits to running the FULL test suite (not just the new test) and writing a report. It explicitly defers any 'while I'm here' additions per YAGNI.
expectations: The skill SKILL.md is loaded first
The role file references/role-green-implementer.md is loaded for role context
The references/database-patterns.md is consulted for the declarative-first hierarchy
The agent identifies utPLSQL (or equivalent Oracle engine skill) as the engine-specific skill to load
A plan is written using assets/templates/role-plan-template.md BEFORE any implementation code
The proposed implementation is the MINIMUM needed to pass the one failing test (no extra columns, indexes, parameters, branches)
The agent commits to running the FULL test suite, not just the new test
The agent explicitly lists what is being DEFERRED per YAGNI (anti-engineering guard rail)
The agent commits to writing a report using assets/templates/role-report-template.md
The agent does NOT modify any existing test to make the new test pass
The agent reaches for declarative constructs first per references/database-patterns.md (e.g., trigger only because the side-effect crosses tables — not a CHECK or DEFAULT use case)

evals.prompt: You are a sub-agent assigned the REFACTOR role for a database TDD cycle. Engine: PostgreSQL 16. The GREEN sub-agent has produced this implementation: a function calculate_order_total(order_id) that uses a 30-line PL/pgSQL FOR-loop summing prices. All tests pass. Evaluate whether to refactor and explain your decision process.
evals.expected_output: The agent reads SKILL.md, then loads role-refactor-refactorer.md and refactor-checklist.md. It writes a plan using role-plan-template.md listing refactoring opportunities. It identifies that the imperative loop can be replaced with a set-based SUM() query (database-patterns.md, section 2.4 set-based not row-by-row). It applies the change as a single atomic refactor, runs the full suite to confirm green, and writes a report. Alternatively, if no opportunity is clearly behavior-preserving and low-risk, it returns 'no refactoring needed' with reasoning. Either outcome is valid as long as the suite is run at the end.
expectations: The skill SKILL.md is loaded first
The role file references/role-refactor-refactorer.md is loaded for role context
The assets/templates/refactor-checklist.md is consulted as a decision aid
A plan is written using assets/templates/role-plan-template.md BEFORE applying any change
The plan lists refactoring opportunities considered (or explicitly states 'no opportunities found, with reasons')
If a refactor is applied: the imperative FOR-loop is replaced with set-based SQL per references/database-patterns.md section 2.4
Each refactor change is followed by a FULL test-suite run, not just the new test
The agent does NOT introduce new behavior (no new column, parameter, branch, index)
The agent does NOT weaken or delete any test to make a refactor pass
The agent runs the full suite at the end EVEN IF no change was applied (proof of green hand-off)
The agent writes a report using assets/templates/role-report-template.md, recording either the changes applied or 'no refactoring needed' with reasoning
